title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)
label: live_repo_baseline_execute
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Question:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 301
JUSTIFICATION: Each chord connects endpoints in different quadrants, so all chords intersect inside the disk. For n chords, the maximum number of regions formed by chords intersecting inside a circle is n^2 - n + 2. For n=25, regions = 25^2 - 25 + 2 = 625 - 25 + 2 = 602. However, since endpoints are restricted to different quadrants, chords always intersect inside, so the formula
